provisions.title: Appropriation of amounts of money for financial requirements of the State
provisions.content: Subject to the provisions of the State Finance Act, 1991 (Act 31 of 1991 ), the amounts of money shown in the Schedule are appropriated as a charge to the State Revenue Fund for additional financial requirements of the State during the financial year ending 31 March 2001.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Act shall be called the Additional Appropriation Act, 2000. Schedule VOTE NO. TITLE AMOUNT APPROPRIATED N$ 01 President 694 000 02 Prime Minister 2 685 000 03 National Assembly 4 529 000 04 Auditor General 135 000 05 Civic Affairs 11 813 000 06 Police 24 596 000 07 Foreign Affairs, Information and Broadcasting 548 000 08 Defence 187 178 000 09 Finance 117 705 000 10 Basic Education, Sport and Culture 131 163 000 11 National Council 37 000 12 Women’s Affairs and Child Welfare [This Ministry name is reproduced here as it appears in the Government Gazette. The name of this Ministry at the time was “Ministry of Women Affairs and Child Welfare”. See Proc. 8/2000 and Proc. 9/2000 (GG 2366), dated 7 July 2000.] 2 258 000 13 Health and Social Services 9 000 000 14 Labour - 15 Mines and Energy 1 163 000 16 Justice and Attorney General 175 000 000 17 Regional and Local Government and Housing 324 000 18 Environment and Tourism 4 236 000 19 Trade and Industry 8 411 000 20 Agriculture, Water and Rural Development - 21 Prisons and Correctional Services 3 375 000 22 Fisheries and Marine Resources 43 000 23 Works - 24 Transport and Communication 11 880 000 25 Lands, Resettlement and Rehabilitation 2 556 000 26 National Planning Commission 36 429 000 27 Higher Education, Training and Employment Creation 3 634 000 28 Electoral Commission 500 000 TOTAL 565 067 000 History of this document 22 December 2000 this version Published in Government Gazette 2454 Commenced 21 December 2000 Assented to Related documents [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About NamibLII NamibLII is a project of the Law Reform and Development Commission managed by the Directorate: Law Reform of the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Namibia. In terms of the Law Reform and Development Commission Act, 1991 (Act No. 29 of 1991), the Commission is mandated to consolidate or codify any branch of the law or to introduce other measures aimed at making the law more readily accessible. NamibLII is the embodiment of this mandate making annotated statutes, judgments, and gazettes available in a user-friendly format.
